windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: fork @ bbfc54d9. MultiStat histogram CRASH FIXED: host int-div-by-zero in the histogram grid sizing (numBlocks.x *= CeilDivide(N, x*y*z) divided by zero when a dim was 0, e.g. partCount==0, before the IsGridEmpty guard); fixed with a guarded ScaleBlockCountToOccupancy helper (hist.cuh) at all 16 launch sites in hist_binary/half_byte/one_byte.cu + hist_2_one_byte_base.cuh, bit-identical to the old code for non-empty grids. gfx1151 GPU-validated: cuda_util-ut 48/48, gpu_data-ut 20/20, methods-ut histograms 8/8 + exact-leaves 15/15 + multistat CheckHistograms (GPU-vs-CPU histogram values) PASS (WithoutOneHot2/17 pass end-to-end); targets-ut TCombinationTargetTests 3/3 (exercises dcg.cu) + Huber/MultiLogit/Tweedie pass. Langevin proven NOT a ROCm defect (won't fix). OPEN BLOCKER (separate subsystem, NOT the histogram): TPointwiseMultiStatHistogramTest still fails 2 of its configs (WithoutOneHot1, WithOneHot3) in the POST-SPLIT path -- CheckAndMakeSplit AssertVecEqual on reordered indices (cpu 37888 vs gpu 0). Pre-existing, masked by the crash, unaffected by the histogram fix; gather/split path (gather_bins/split_points), candidate wave32 scan/scatter. TAucTest/QueryCrossEntropy + the 2M-doc FatSplitPropsTest are just slow on the APU (no failure). full-app e2e not run. See notes.md 2026-06-02.
